keyPoints: Data Control Language (DCL): Used for Security and Access Control
Data Definition Language (DDL): Used for Creating, Modifying, and Dropping Database Objects
Data Manipulation Language (DML): Used for Inserting, Updating, and Deleting Data
Data Control Language (DCL): Used for Granting Privileges, Creating Users, and Dropping Users
SQL (Structured Query Language): Used for Interacting with the Database
Normalization: Normalizing the Database to Reduce Data Redundancy and Improve Data Integrity
